name: supernatural_beliefs
description: Measures the breadth and depth of an individual's belief in supernatural, religious, and metaphysical entities or forces, ranging from orthodox Christian beliefs (God, hell, resurrection) to folk and syncretic beliefs (witchcraft, karma, limpias, reincarnation). Captures the overall cosmological worldview rather than institutional religiosity.

name: personal_religiosity
description: Measures the degree to which religion is personally meaningful and actively practiced in daily life, including prayer outside formal worship, finding comfort in religion, using religious values to guide family behavior, reliance on clergy for life decisions, and the importance of God in one's life. Reflects internalized religious commitment rather than mere affiliation.

name: religious_socialization
description: Measures the extent to which an individual's religious identity and practice were shaped by family and institutional upbringing, including whether they share the religion of their parents, whether family members are religiously homogeneous, and whether they received formal religious education. Captures intergenerational transmission of religion.

name: church_state_separation
description: Measures attitudes toward the appropriate boundaries between religious institutions and the public sphere, including opinions on clergy discussing politics during services, churches forming political parties, use of public resources for religious events, church taxation, and media broadcasting religious content. Reflects support for or opposition to laicidad (secularism) as a political principle.

name: religious_tolerance
description: Measures the degree to which individuals accept religious diversity in their social environment, including willingness to cohabit with people of other religions, family tolerance toward non-Catholics, justification for expelling religious minorities from communities, and opinions on how authorities should handle majority-minority religious conflicts.
